verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc locutus sum vobis, ut non scandalizemini.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἵνα μὴ σκανδαλισθῆτε
verses.king_james: These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be offended.

verses.latin_vulgate: Absque synagogis facient vos: sed venit hora, ut omnis qui interficit vos arbitretur obsequium se præstare Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀποσυναγώγους ποιήσουσιν ὑμᾶς ἀλλ᾽ ἔρχεται ὥρα ἵνα πᾶς ὁ ἀποκτείνας ὑμᾶς δόξῃ λατρείαν προσφέρειν τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever killeth you will think that he doeth God service.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et hæc facient vobis, quia non noverunt Patrem, neque me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταῦτα ποιήσουσιν ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐκ ἔγνωσαν τὸν πατέρα οὐδὲ ἐμέ
verses.king_james: And these things will they do unto you, because they have not known the Father, nor me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed hæc locutus sum vobis, ut cum venerit hora eorum, reminiscamini quia ego dixi vobis. Hæc autem vobis ab initio non dixi, quia vobiscum eram.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἵνα ὅταν ἔλθῃ ἡ ὥρα μνημονεύητε αὐτῶν ὅτι ἐγὼ εἶπον ὑμῖν ταῦτα δὲ ὑμῖν ἐξ ἀρχῆς οὐκ εἶπον ὅτι μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν ἤμην
verses.king_james: But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc vado ad eum qui misit me; et nemo ex vobis interrogat me: Quo vadis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νῦν δὲ ὑπάγω πρὸς τὸν πέμψαντά με καὶ οὐδεὶς ἐξ ὑμῶν ἐρωτᾷ με ποῦ ὑπάγεις
verses.king_james: But now I go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?

verses.latin_vulgate: sed quia hæc locutus sum vobis, tristitia implevit cor vestrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ὅτι ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἡ λύπη πεπλήρωκεν ὑμῶν τὴν καρδίαν
verses.king_james: But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed ego veritatem dico vobis: expedit vobis ut ego vadam: si enim non abiero, Paraclitus non veniet ad vos; si autem abiero, mittam eum ad vos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἐγὼ τὴν ἀλήθειαν λέγω ὑμῖν συμφέρει ὑμῖν ἵνα ἐγὼ ἀπέλθω ἐὰν γὰρ μὴ ἀπέλθω ὁ παράκλητος οὐκ ἐλεύσεται πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐὰν δὲ πορευθῶ πέμψω αὐτὸν πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum venerit ille, arguet mundum de peccato, et de justitia, et de judicio.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθὼν ἐκεῖνος ἐλέγξει τὸν κόσμον περὶ ἁμαρτίας καὶ περὶ δικαιοσύνης καὶ περὶ κρίσεως
verses.king_james: And when he is come, he will reprove the world of sin, and of righteousness, and of judgment:

verses.latin_vulgate: De peccato quidem, quia non crediderunt in me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ ἁμαρτίας μέν ὅτι οὐ πιστεύουσιν εἰς ἐμέ
verses.king_james: Of sin, because they believe not on me;

verses.latin_vulgate: De justitia vero, quia ad Patrem vado, et jam non videbitis me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ δικαιοσύνης δέ ὅτι πρὸς τὸν πατέρα μου ὑπάγω καὶ οὐκ ἔτι θεωρεῖτέ με
verses.king_james: Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more;

verses.latin_vulgate: De judicio autem, quia princeps hujus mundi jam judicatus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ δὲ κρίσεως ὅτι ὁ ἄρχων τοῦ κόσμου τούτου κέκριται
verses.king_james: Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adhuc multa habeo vobis dicere, sed non potestis portare modo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔτι πολλὰ ἔχω λέγειν ὑμῖν ἀλλ᾽ οὐ δύνασθε βαστάζειν ἄρτι
verses.king_james: I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venerit ille Spiritus veritatis, docebit vos omnem veritatem: non enim loquetur a semetipso, sed quæcumque audiet loquetur, et quæ ventura sunt annuntiabit vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἔλθῃ ἐκεῖνος τὸ πνεῦμα τῆς ἀληθείας ὁδηγήσει ὑμᾶς εἰς πάσαν τῆν ἀληθείαν οὐ γὰρ λαλήσει ἀφ᾽ ἑαυτοῦ ἀλλ᾽ ὅσα ἂν ἀκούσῃ λαλήσει καὶ τὰ ἐρχόμενα ἀναγγελεῖ ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Howbeit when he, the Spirit of truth, is come, he will guide you into all truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, *that* shall he speak: and he will shew you things to come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ille me clarificabit, quia de meo accipiet, et annuntiabit vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκεῖνος ἐμὲ δοξάσει ὅτι ἐκ τοῦ ἐμοῦ λήψεται, καὶ ἀναγγελεῖ ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: He shall glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew *it* unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia quæcumque habet Pater, mea sunt. Propterea dixi: quia de meo accipiet, et annuntiabit vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα ὅσα ἔχει ὁ πατὴρ ἐμά ἐστιν διὰ τοῦτο εἶπον ὅτι ἐκ τοῦ ἐμοῦ λήψεται, καὶ ἀναγγελεῖ ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: All things that the Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew *it* unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Modicum, et jam non videbitis me; et iterum modicum, et videbitis me: quia vado ad Patrem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μικρὸν καὶ οὐ θεωρεῖτέ με καὶ πάλιν μικρὸν καὶ ὄψεσθέ με ὅτι ἐγὼ ὑπάγω πρὸς τὸν πατέρα
verses.king_james: A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me, because I go to the Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo ex discipulis ejus ad invicem: Quid est hoc quod dicit nobis: Modicum, et non videbitis me; et iterum modicum, et videbitis me, et quia vado ad Patrem?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὖν ἐκ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ πρὸς ἀλλήλους τί ἐστιν τοῦτο ὃ λέγει ἡμῖν μικρὸν καὶ οὐ θεωρεῖτέ με καὶ πάλιν μικρὸν καὶ ὄψεσθέ με καί ὅτι ἐγὼ ὑπάγω πρὸς τὸν πατέρα
verses.king_james: Then said *some* of his disciples among themselves, What is this that he saith unto us, a little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me: and, Because I go to the Father?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant ergo: Quid est hoc quod dicit: Modicum? nescimus quid loquitur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον οὖν τοῦτο τί ἐστιν ὃ λέγει τὸ μικρόν οὐκ οἴδαμεν τί λαλεῖ
verses.king_james: They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cognovit autem Jesus, quia volebant eum interrogare, et dixit eis: De hoc quæritis inter vos quia dixi: Modicum, et non videbitis me; et iterum modicum, et videbitis me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔγνω οὖν ὁ ἰησοῦς ὅτι ἤθελον αὐτὸν ἐρωτᾶν καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς περὶ τούτου ζητεῖτε μετ᾽ ἀλλήλων ὅτι εἶπον μικρὸν καὶ οὐ θεωρεῖτέ με καὶ πάλιν μικρὸν καὶ ὄψεσθέ με
verses.king_james: Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, Do ye enquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen, amen dico vobis: quia plorabitis, et flebitis vos, mundus autem gaudebit; vos autem contristabimini, sed tristitia vestra vertetur in gaudium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι κλαύσετε καὶ θρηνήσετε ὑμεῖς ὁ δὲ κόσμος χαρήσεται ὑμεῖς δὲ λυπηθήσεσθε ἀλλ᾽ ἡ λύπη ὑμῶν εἰς χαρὰν γενήσεται
verses.king_james: Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your sorrow shall be turned into joy.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mulier cum parit, tristitiam habet, quia venit hora ejus; cum autem pepererit puerum, jam non meminit pressuræ propter gaudium, quia natus est homo in mundum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡ γυνὴ ὅταν τίκτῃ λύπην ἔχει ὅτι ἦλθεν ἡ ὥρα αὐτῆς ὅταν δὲ γεννήσῃ τὸ παιδίον οὐκ ἔτι μνημονεύει τῆς θλίψεως διὰ τὴν χαρὰν ὅτι ἐγεννήθη ἄνθρωπος εἰς τὸν κόσμον
verses.king_james: A woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vos igitur nunc quidem tristitiam habetis, iterum autem videbo vos, et gaudebit cor vestrum: et gaudium vestrum nemo tollet a vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑμεῖς οὖν λύπην μὲν νῦν ἔχετε πάλιν δὲ ὄψομαι ὑμᾶς καὶ χαρήσεται ὑμῶν ἡ καρδία καὶ τὴν χαρὰν ὑμῶν οὐδεὶς αἴρει ἀφ᾽ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And ye now therefore have sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your joy no man taketh from you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et in illo die me non rogabitis quidquam. Amen, amen dico vobis: si quid petieritis Patrem in nomine meo, dabit vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ἐμὲ οὐκ ἐρωτήσετε οὐδέν ἀμὴν ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ὅσα ἄν αἰτήσητε τὸν πατέρα ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου δώσει ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: And in that day ye shall ask me nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall ask the Father in my name, he will give *it* you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Usque modo non petistis quidquam in nomine meo: petite, et accipietis, ut gaudium vestrum sit plenum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕως ἄρτι οὐκ ᾐτήσατε οὐδὲν ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου αἰτεῖτε καὶ λήψεσθε, ἵνα ἡ χαρὰ ὑμῶν ᾖ πεπληρωμένη
verses.king_james: Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc in proverbiis locutus sum vobis. Venit hora cum jam non in proverbiis loquar vobis, sed palam de Patre annuntiabo vobis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα ἐν παροιμίαις λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἀλλ᾽ ἔρχεται ὥρα ὅτε οὐκ ἔτι ἐν παροιμίαις λαλήσω ὑμῖν ἀλλὰ παῤῥησίᾳ περὶ τοῦ πατρὸς ἀναγγελῶ ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: These things have I spoken unto you in proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: in illo die in nomine meo petetis: et non dico vobis quia ego rogabo Patrem de vobis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί μου αἰτήσεσθε καὶ οὐ λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ἐγὼ ἐρωτήσω τὸν πατέρα περὶ ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:

verses.latin_vulgate: ipse enim Pater amat vos, quia vos me amastis, et credidistis, quia ego a Deo exivi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτὸς γὰρ ὁ πατὴρ φιλεῖ ὑμᾶς ὅτι ὑμεῖς ἐμὲ πεφιλήκατε καὶ πεπιστεύκατε ὅτι ἐγὼ παρὰ τοῦ θεοῦ ἐξῆλθον
verses.king_james: For the Father himself loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have believed that I came out from God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exivi a Patre, et veni in mundum: iterum relinquo mundum, et vado ad Patrem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξῆλθον παρὰ τοῦ πατρὸς καὶ ἐλήλυθα εἰς τὸν κόσμον πάλιν ἀφίημι τὸν κόσμον καὶ πορεύομαι πρὸς τὸν πατέρα
verses.king_james: I came forth from the Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicunt ei discipuli ejus: Ecce nunc palam loqueris, et proverbium nullum dicis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγουσιν αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἴδε νῦν παῤῥησίᾳ λαλεῖς καὶ παροιμίαν οὐδεμίαν λέγεις
verses.king_james: His disciples said unto him, Lo, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no proverb.

verses.latin_vulgate: nunc scimus quia scis omnia, et non opus est tibi ut quis te interroget: in hoc credimus quia a Deo existi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νῦν οἴδαμεν ὅτι οἶδας πάντα καὶ οὐ χρείαν ἔχεις ἵνα τίς σε ἐρωτᾷ ἐν τούτῳ πιστεύομεν ὅτι ἀπὸ θεοῦ ἐξῆλθες
verses.king_james: Now are we sure that thou knowest all things, and needest not that any man should ask thee: by this we believe that thou camest forth from God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit eis Jesus: Modo creditis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς ἄρτι πιστεύετε
verses.king_james: Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?

verses.latin_vulgate: ecce venit hora, et jam venit, ut dispergamini unusquisque in propria, et me solum relinquatis: et non sum solus, quia Pater mecum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδού, ἔρχεται ὥρα καὶ νῦν ἐλήλυθεν ἵνα σκορπισθῆτε ἕκαστος εἰς τὰ ἴδια καὶ ἐμὲ μόνον ἀφῆτε καὶ οὐκ εἰμὶ μόνος ὅτι ὁ πατὴρ μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc locutus sum vobis, ut in me pacem habeatis. In mundo pressuram habebitis: sed confidite, ego vici mundum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα λελάληκα ὑμῖν ἵνα ἐν ἐμοὶ εἰρήνην ἔχητε ἐν τῷ κόσμῳ θλῖψιν ἔχετε ἀλλὰ θαρσεῖτε ἐγὼ νενίκηκα τὸν κόσμον
verses.king_james: These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have peace. In the world ye shall have tribulation: but be of good cheer; I have overcome the world.
